title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman meets heads of banks on AI risks following concerns over Anthropic's Mythos
description: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman convened a meeting with bank heads to address Artificial Intelligence (AI) risks, particularly concerning Anthropic's Mythos model and its potential to compromise financial system data security. Banks have been urged to implement preemptive measures to safeguard their systems, customer data, and funds following discussions on AI's implications for the financial sector.

title: Samsung workers demand higher pay
description: Thousands of Samsung Electronics workers rallied Thursday at its computer chip complex in Pyeongtaek, South Korea, demanding higher bonuses and threatening to strike as booming demand for artificial intelligence drives up memory-chip profits.

title: China’s DeepSeek looks to tap external investors including Alibaba, Tencent
description: The Chinese artificial-intelligence startup is tapping external investors for the first time to raise funds for research and development, people familiar with the matter said.
content:
In recent weeks, DeepSeek has held talks with potential investors, including Chinese technology giants Alibaba and Tencent, seeking to raise at least $300 million, the people said. The funds will be denominated in Chinese yuan, they said.

title: Semiconductor boom drives South Korea to fastest economic growth in 5 years
description: South Korea recorded its most significant economic expansion in more than five years during the first quarter, fueled by a global surge in demand for semiconductors and artificial intelligence technology.
content:
Seoul [South Korea], April 23 (ANI): South Korea recorded its most significant economic expansion in more than five years during the first quarter, fueled by a global surge in demand for semiconductors and artificial intelligence technology.

title: Exclusive-SpaceX IPO filing shows Elon Musk can retain board control
description: SAN FRANCISCO / BOSTON, April 23 (Reuters) - SpaceX is telling prospective investors its board will not need a majority of ⁠directors who are independent of the company, according to an excerpt of its IPO filing reviewed by ‌Reuters, underscoring how founder Elon Musk is retaining control of the rocket and artificial intelligence maker.
